emergency_definition: fire alarm triggered or system failure

order: If no answer, call Linda Martinez (service manager)
emergency_routing_rules.fallback: Inform caller someone will call back within 30 minutes

non_emergency_routing_rules: Take detailed message with customer name, phone, and service request. Follow up next business day by 10 AM.

call_transfer_rules.message_if_fails: Our team has been notified and will call you back within 30 minutes

integration_constraints: Never create jobs automatically in ServiceTrade — dispatcher must do this manually after call
All emergency calls must be logged in ServiceTrade within 15 minutes
after_hours_flow_summary: Greet caller, confirm if emergency. If emergency: collect name, number, address and attempt transfer to Dave. If no answer, assure callback within 30 minutes. If non-emergency: take message for next business day.
office_hours_flow_summary: Greet caller, collect name and phone number, determine if emergency vs non-emergency, route accordingly to Dave or ask for message
questions_or_unknowns: Exact phone numbers for Dave and Linda not confirmed in demo call
Transfer timeout duration not explicitly confirmed — assumed 45 seconds based on industry standard
Unclear if customer wants callback within 30 min for ALL emergencies or only when transfer fails
notes: Client strongly emphasized ServiceTrade constraint. Demo call was directional and high-level. Participant was operations manager, not dispatcher.
